Caption: X-ray computational tomography images (a) without and (b) with an imaging solid electrolyte particle. The applied pressure was 10 MPa.
(a) Stress–strain characteristics of solid electrolytes comprising volume fractions of Li6PS5Cl:Li6PS5Cl0.5I0.5 of 100:0, 50:50, and 0:100. (b) XRD pattern of Li6PS5Cl0.5I0.5.

(a) Increase in ratio of contact area to applied pressure (dR c /dp) as a function of initial contact ratio (R c ). Particle X-ray computational tomography three-dimensional images (topside) and two-dimensional CT raw images (bottom side) of (b) low and (c) high initial contact ratio particles (yellow: non-contact area; purple: contact area). (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)

(a) Increase in ratio of sphericity to applied pressure (dS p /dp) as a function of initial sphericity (S p ). Three-dimensional images and two-dimensional images of X-ray computational tomography of particles with (b) low and (c) high initial sphericity (left-side images show surface rendering; middle images show sliced depictions; right-side images show two-dimensional raw CT image).
